basic_info.headline: Making teams better with empathy, thoughtfulness, and cultural design
basic_info.about:
I'm engineer always looking for tough problems to solve, looking to marry elegant systems to human-oriented problems. More recently, I've been practicing both organizational design (incentive structures, communication + coordination networks; how high-performing teams form, fail to form, and disintegrate) and the application of empathy + nonviolent communication to the workplace.

I aim to anchor my teams by asking questions like:
- What is the most value we could deliver in 1 week vs 2 years? How do we set ourselves up for that success? How do we preemptively account for potential failure?
- How can this meeting be made most useful to its participants?
- What data or value difference leads to this disagreement?

experience.title: Staff Software Engineer
experience.description: Built the Credit Management product (since deprecated) and the core customer experience. Responsible for ingesting project data from vendors and suppliers to present a consolidated "canonical" view of projects in the VCM.

experience.title: Software Engineering
experience.description: Tech Lead for a team of 6 engineers for 2 years. Owned and upgraded core systems in the payroll product to keep up to date with latest tax legislation. Increased throughput of daily business critical ACH processes to buy 2 years of runway. Closed compliance gaps impacting a majority of employees and contractors paid through Gusto. Flexed to cover product, design, and management responsibilities.

experience.title: Software Engineer
experience.description:
Develop and continuously refactor a Postgres/Rails/React application, extracting isolated functionality and improving test coverage.
Participate in the Diversity, Hiring Process, and Enablement recruiting initiatives, revamping the interview process via documentation and training sessions
Designed and ran a Pair Programming Workshop for my team
Drove conversations with each cohort of direct reports to my engineering manager to explicitly establish expectations and needs in both directions

experience.title: Software Engineer
experience.description:
Developed in and continuously refactored a Rails/Ember application, a Java microservice, and the Square Register iOS and Android native applications, improving test quality and coverage
Wrote design documents to discuss and record engineering decisions
Participated in a week-long on-call rotation, being first responder to any and all customer issues
One of ~30 Hiring Barometers, conducting onsite and phone interviews (137 in 2016, 115 in Jan-June 2017) and making hiring decisions for incoming engineering candidates.

projects.name: PianoScribe
